hero: Brooklyn is brownstones and basement bars, summer stoops and snow days. Walk the bridge once at dawn — that's the whole pitch.

sections.title: Neighbourhoods to know
sections.body:
Fort Greene for tree-lined streets and Sunday at the Greenmarket.
Williamsburg for wine bars and waterfront sunsets.
Prospect Heights for the museum, the park, and quieter bagels.

sections.title: Getting around
sections.body:
Tap any contactless card on the OMNY readers; no MetroCard needed.
CitiBike is everywhere — day pass available in the app.
Uber/Lyft work; yellow cabs are mostly Manhattan.

sections.title: Eat
sections.body:
Pizza: L'industrie (Williamsburg).
Bagels: Apollo (Greenpoint).
Sunday roast: The Finch (closed) → try Olmsted instead.
Late-night bowl: Lucky Dog ramen.

sections.title: Local customs
sections.body:
Tipping is 18–20%, even on takeout.
Don't stop in the middle of the sidewalk.
Food-cart cash is appreciated; cards are fine but slow up the line.

sections.title: If something goes wrong
sections.body:
Emergency 911.
Non-emergency 311 (city services).
Mt. Sinai Brooklyn: +1 718 252 3000 for ER.
